steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits between the noun "term" and the clause "that this specimen serves as the basis...". The blank must link the noun to that clause by modifying "term" — a participle is needed here, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is indicating': A finite verb (present progressive) that would make the clause a separate sentence fragment requiring a subject. ✗
  • Choice B — 'has indicated': A finite verb (present perfect) with the same problem — no subject and creates an ungrammatical structure. ✗
  • Choice C — 'indicated': A past participle suggesting the term once indicated but no longer does, which distorts the meaning. ✗
  • Choice D — 'indicating': A present participle that correctly modifies "term," producing a term indicating that this specimen serves as the basis for all formal scientific descriptions of the species. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
